Class BaseBlue

java.lang.Object
xyz.yaszu.freedom.Soul.Base.BaseBlue
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.bukkit.event.Listener, Base_Soul
public class BaseBlue extends Object implements Base_Soul, org.bukkit.event.Listener
The BaseBlue class represents a derived implementation of the Base_Soul class providing specific functionality related to abilities, descriptions, and interactions for a particular type of resource or ability container. This class might be designed for use in a game or application involving player-based or item-based actions.

  • Method Details

    • Name_For_Container

      public String Name_For_Container()
      Returns the name of the container associated with this instance.
      Specified by:
      Name_For_Container in interface Base_Soul
      Returns:
      A string representing the name of the container, in this case "BaseBlue".

    • Name

      public net.kyori.adventure.text.Component Name()
      Returns a formatted component representing the name, constructed using MiniMessage.
      Specified by:
      Name in interface Base_Soul
      Returns:
      A Component instance representing the name, in this case formatted as "Blue".

    • Description

      public net.kyori.adventure.text.Component Description()
      Constructs a descriptive component based on a predefined reverse-encoded message. The message is processed and returned as a formatted text component using the MiniMessage system.
      Specified by:
      Description in interface Base_Soul
      Returns:
      A Component object representing the formatted description derived from the encoded message.

    • Icon

      public org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack Icon()
      Returns an ItemStack representing the icon associated with this instance.
      Specified by:
      Icon in interface Base_Soul
      Returns:
      An ItemStack of type Material.CLOCK.

    • AbilityOneName

      public net.kyori.adventure.text.Component AbilityOneName()
      Constructs and returns a labeled component representing the name of the first ability. The label is formatted using the MiniMessage system, styled as "Ability One - Strings Attached".
      Specified by:
      AbilityOneName in interface Base_Soul
      Returns:
      A Component object containing the formatted name of the first ability.

    • AbilityOneDescription

      public net.kyori.adventure.text.Component AbilityOneDescription()
      Constructs and returns a descriptive component for the first ability.
      Specified by:
      AbilityOneDescription in interface Base_Soul
      Returns:
      A Component object containing the formatted description of the first ability.

    • AbilityOne

      public void AbilityOne(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Triggers the first ability for the specified player. Primarily serves as a simplified version of the overloaded method, delegating its execution.
      Specified by:
      AbilityOne in interface Base_Soul
      Parameters:
      player - The player who will activate the first ability.

    • AbilityOne

      public void AbilityOne(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, boolean is_imbue)
      Handles the execution of the first ability for a player.
      Specified by:
      AbilityOne in interface Base_Soul
      Parameters:
      player - Player to handle Ability One for
      is_imbue - checking if it's imbued

    • Related_Item

      public org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack Related_Item()
      Returns the related item for this instance.
      Specified by:
      Related_Item in interface Base_Soul
      Returns:
      An ItemStack representing the related item, in this case Material.CLOCK.

    • AbilityTwoName

      public net.kyori.adventure.text.Component AbilityTwoName()
      Returns the name of the second ability.
      Specified by:
      AbilityTwoName in interface Base_Soul
      Returns:
      A Component representing the name of the second ability.

    • AbilityTwoDescription

      public net.kyori.adventure.text.Component AbilityTwoDescription()
      Constructs and returns a descriptive component for the second ability. The description provides details about the ability triggered by the anti clock item, where Blue can set a teleport location.
      Specified by:
      AbilityTwoDescription in interface Base_Soul
      Returns:
      A Component object containing the formatted description of the second ability.

    • load

      public void load(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Initiates a teleportation process for the specified player to a saved location. This method implements visual effects during the loading process and allows nearby trusted players to be teleported with the initiating player. The teleportation is canceled if certain conditions fail, such as player movement, exceeding the allowed distance, or health increase during the process.
      Parameters:
      player - The player who is initiating the teleportation, whose persistent data container holds the saved location and necessary data for teleportation.

    • threes

      public static double threes(int num)
      Checks if a number is a multiple of 3, 2, or 1.
      Parameters:
      num - The number to check.
      Returns:
      3 if the number is a multiple of 3, 2 if it is a multiple of 2, and 1 otherwise.

    • save

      public void save(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Saves the current location of the player to a persistent data container.
      Parameters:
      player - The player whose location is to be saved.
